title: orders-api latency spike and 503s during traffic surge
error_signature: 503 Service Unavailable from orders-api; DB connection pool acquire timeout
summary: orders-api p99 latency jumped from ~120ms to >5s and it began returning 503s around 09:15 UTC, coinciding with a marketing-campaign traffic surge. orders-db reports it is healthy.

ground_truth.root_cause: orders-api's client-side connection pool to orders-db (max 20) was exhausted under the traffic surge. New requests block waiting for a free connection, exceed the 5s acquire timeout, and return 503. The database itself has headroom (42/100 connections) — the bottleneck is the undersized client pool, worsened by a slow unindexed query holding connections longer.
key_evidence: orders-api log: 'could not acquire DB connection from pool within 5000ms (active=20/20, waiting=37)'
orders-db log: 'connection count 42/100 — within limits' (the DB is not the limit; the client pool is)
trace tr-1002-a: GET /orders spends ~4980ms in the 'db.acquire' span and never reaches the query
expected_fix_themes: increase the orders-api DB pool size (and DB max_connections) to match real concurrency
shorten transaction scope / fix slow query (add index on orders.user_id) so connections return faster
add backpressure or autoscaling and tune the acquire timeout so a surge degrades gracefully
